title: Forcepoint ONE SSE Web Security Events (Forcepoint)
dataSources: Forcepoint ONE SSE logs via Splunkbase app
spl:
index=proxy sourcetype="forcepoint:one" earliest=-7d
| where action IN ("block", "deny")
| stats count by policy_name, category, user, action
| sort -count
description: Forcepoint ONE SSE delivers cloud-native SWG, CASB, and ZTNA from a unified platform. Web security events reveal policy violations, threat blocks, and shadow IT usage across all users.
value: Data protection teams unify Forcepoint ONE SSE DLP events across all channels (web, cloud, email, endpoint) into a single view, detecting cross-channel exfiltration where the same user triggers DLP violations through multiple exit paths.
implementation: Install the Forcepoint Insights SIEM App and configure log ingestion. Map categories to your acceptable-use policy. Alert on high-risk category blocks. Report on enforcement effectiveness.
visualization: Bar chart (blocks by category), Table (top users blocked), Line chart (block trend), Pie chart (policy distribution).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
  by All_Traffic.user, All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest span=1h
| sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Spikes from new SaaS rollouts, automated workflows, sanctioned bulk uploads, or M&A integration.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Forcepoint ONE SSE DLP events in `index=forcepoint` with `sourcetype=forcepoint:one:sse` or `sourcetype=forcepoint:dlp:incidents`. Key fields: `action` (block/monitor/quarantine/encrypt), `policy_name`, `channel` (Web/Cloud/Email/Endpoint), `user`, `severity`, `data_pattern`, `file_name`, `destination`, `match_count`.
• Forcepoint ONE SSE unifies DLP across channels: web (SWG), cloud apps (CASB), email, and endpoint. This UC provides a single view across all DLP channels — unlike UC-17.5.x which covered individual Forcepoint products.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify unified DLP events:
```spl
index=forcepoint earliest=-7d
| where isnotnull(data_pattern) AND isnotnull(channel)
| stats count by channel, action, severity
```

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — Cross-channel DLP:
```spl
index=forcepoint earliest=-7d
| where isnotnull(data_pattern)
| stats count sum(match_count) as total_matches dc(user) as users by data_pattern, channel, action
| sort - total_matches
```

Understanding this SPL: Groups DLP violations by data pattern (PII, PCI, HIPAA) and channel. When the same data pattern appears across multiple channels — the same person sending PCI data via web upload and email — it indicates a systematic data handling issue, not an accident.

Cross-channel exfiltration detection:
```spl
index=forcepoint earliest=-7d
| where isnotnull(data_pattern) AND action IN ("block","quarantine")
| stats dc(channel) as channels values(channel) as channel_list count by user, data_pattern
| where channels > 1
| sort - channels
```

Step 3 — Validate
(a) Trigger a DLP policy via web upload and email. Verify both appear.
(b) In Forcepoint ONE: Analytics > DLP Incidents.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: Row 1 — DLP by channel. Row 2 — Cross-channel users. Row 3 — Severity trend.
Alert: When user triggers DLP in 2+ channels within 24h.

Runbook:
1. **Cross-channel DLP** — Investigate user for intentional exfiltration.
2. **Block on PCI** — Verify data was actually blocked. Check destination.
3. **Monitor-only on sensitive data** — Upgrade to block.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Only one channel showing** — Other channels may not forward to Splunk.
• **`data_pattern` generic** — Custom patterns may not have descriptive names.
grandmaExplanation: Forcepoint ONE watches for sensitive data leaving through every exit — web, email, cloud apps. When the same person triggers a data warning across multiple exits, that is not accidental — they are looking for a way out with that data, and we need to stop them.
hardware: Forcepoint ONE (cloud), Forcepoint ONE Smart Edge Agent (endpoint)
